full_name: Stochastic Mirror Descent (Convex, Variable Step Size)
source_reference: Lan, First-order and Stochastic Optimization Methods for Machine Learning, Chapter 3-4 (Theorem 4.1)
archetype_note: Novel proof structure. Requires Bregman divergence infrastructure not present in Mathlib. No Layer 1 meta-theorem available. Must build V(x,z), prox-mapping, and three-point lemma from scratch.
prerequisite_lib_work: Define distance generating function v : X -> R (strongly convex, differentiable)
Define Bregman divergence V(x, z) = v(z) - [v(x) + <∇v(x), z - x>]
Prove V(x,z) ≥ 0 (from strong convexity of v)
Define prox-mapping: argmin_{x ∈ X} γ⟨g, x⟩ + V(z, x)

constraint_set.description: Closed convex set X ⊆ E (finite-dimensional or Banach space)
constraint_set.note: The algorithm requires a prox-friendly set X — one for which the argmin in the update rule is tractable.

distance_generating_function.description: v : X° → R is a distance generating function (prox-function), 1-strongly convex with respect to ‖·‖
distance_generating_function.example: Simplest example: v(x) = ‖x‖₂²/2, which gives V(x,z) = ‖x - z‖₂²/2 (reduces to SGD/subgradient)
distance_generating_function.note: X° is the domain of v (may be larger than X)

bregman_divergence.description: Bregman divergence (prox-function) V : X° × X → ℝ₊
bregman_divergence.definition_math: V(x, z) = v(z) - [v(x) + \langle \nabla v(x),\, z - x \rangle]
bregman_divergence.key_property: V(x, z) ≥ 0 for all x ∈ X°, z ∈ X (from 1-strong convexity of v)
bregman_divergence.note: V is not symmetric in general. V(x, z) measures how much z deviates from the first-order approximation of v at x.

objective.subgradient_condition: g_t ∈ ∂f(x_t): a subgradient of f at x_t
objective.note: f need not be smooth. The algorithm only requires access to a subgradient oracle.

stochastic_oracle.description: At each step t, the algorithm observes a stochastic gradient G_t = g_t + δ_t
lean_hypotheses: E[‖δ_t‖_*²] ≤ σ²  (bounded variance in dual norm)
stochastic_oracle.note: δ_t = G_t - g_t is the noise term. The dual norm ‖·‖_* is required because G_t lives in the dual space.

oracle_magnitude.hypothesis: ‖G_t‖_* ≤ M  (almost surely, or: E[‖G_t‖_*²] ≤ M² + σ²)

step_sizes.note: Theorem 4.1 holds for general γ_t. Optimal choice is γ_t = D / (M√T) for constant M and T steps.

initial_point.description: x_1 ∈ X (arbitrary starting point in X)

update_rule.math: x_{t+1} = \operatorname{argmin}_{x \in X}\; \gamma_t \langle G_t(x_t),\, x \rangle + V(x_t,\, x),\quad t = 1, 2, \ldots
update_rule.interpretation: Move to the point in X that minimizes the linearization of f (via stochastic gradient G_t) plus a Bregman proximity penalty to x_t.
update_rule.note: When v(x) = ‖x‖₂²/2, this reduces to projected gradient descent: x_{t+1} = Proj_X(x_t - γ_t G_t).

output.description: Step-size weighted Cesaro average of iterates from step s to k
output.note: Convergence is measured at this average point, not the last iterate.

3_key_lemma_chain.title: Key Lemma Chain
3_key_lemma_chain.overview: The proof proceeds in three stages: (1) single-step bound via Lemma 3.4, (2) aggregate via Young's inequality + oracle bound, (3) sum and rearrange.

lemma_3_4.title: Three-Point Lemma (Descent Lemma for Mirror Descent)
lemma_3_4.statement_math: \gamma_t \langle g(x_t),\, x_{t+1} - x \rangle + V(x_t, x_{t+1}) \leq V(x_t, x) - V(x_{t+1}, x),\quad \forall x \in X
proof_sketch: Step 1: By the optimality condition of (3.2.5), the minimizer x_{t+1} satisfies the first-order KKT condition: <γ_t g(x_t) + ∇v(x_{t+1}) - ∇v(x_t), x - x_{t+1}> ≥ 0 for all x ∈ X.
Step 2: Rearrange to: γ_t <g(x_t), x_{t+1} - x> ≤ <∇v(x_t) - ∇v(x_{t+1}), x - x_{t+1}>

Step 3: Apply the Bregman identity (3.2.6): V(x_t, x) = V(x_t, x_{t+1}) + <∇V(x_t, x_{t+1}), x - x_{t+1}> + V(x_{t+1}, x). This gives <∇v(x_t) - ∇v(x_{t+1}), x - x_{t+1}> = V(x_t, x) - V(x_t, x_{t+1}) - V(x_{t+1}, x).
Step 4: Combine steps 2 and 3 to get the result.
lemma_3_4.key_technique: Optimality condition of prox-mapping + Bregman three-point identity

step_1.title: Single-step suboptimality bound
derivation: Apply Lemma 3.4 with g = G_t (stochastic gradient) and x = x* (optimal point):
Rewrite: γ_t <G_t, x_t - x*> = γ_t <G_t, x_t - x_{t+1}> + γ_t <G_t, x_{t+1} - x*>
Bound γ_t <G_t, x_t - x_{t+1}> using Cauchy-Schwarz and Young's inequality:
(Here we use: ‖x_t - x_{t+1}‖² / 2 ≤ V(x_t, x_{t+1}) from 1-strong convexity of v, and bt - at²/2 ≤ b²/(2a) for a > 0.)
step_1.result_math: \gamma_t [f(x_t) - f(x)] \leq V(x_t, x) - V(x_{t+1}, x) + \gamma_t^2 \|G_t\|_*^2 / 2 - \gamma_t \langle \delta_t, x_t - x \rangle
step_1.note: The convexity of f gives f(x_t) - f(x) ≤ <g_t, x_t - x> = <G_t - δ_t, x_t - x>. The noise term -γ_t <δ_t, x_t - x> appears and is handled in Step 3.

step_2.title: Oracle variance bound
step_2.result_math: \gamma_t [f(x_t) - f(x)] \leq V(x_t, x) - V(x_{t+1}, x) + 2\gamma_t^2(M^2 + \|\delta_t\|_*^2) - \gamma_t \langle \delta_t, x_t - x \rangle

step_3.title: Summation and noise cancellation
derivation: Σ γ_t [f(x_t) - f(x)] ≤ V(x_s, x) - V(x_{k+1}, x) + 2Σ γ_t²(M² + ‖δ_t‖_*²) - Σ γ_t <δ_t, x_t - x>
The V-terms telescope: Σ [V(x_t, x) - V(x_{t+1}, x)] = V(x_s, x) - V(x_{k+1}, x) ≤ V(x_s, x)  (since V ≥ 0).
E[Σ γ_t <δ_t, x_t - x>] = 0  (noise is zero-mean conditional on ξ_{[t-1]}, and x_t is ξ_{[t-1]}-measurable)
step_3.key_technique: Telescoping of V-terms + martingale property of noise (E[<δ_t, x_t - x>] = 0)

step_4.title: Extract Cesaro average
derivation: By convexity of f: f(\bar{x}_s^k) ≤ (Σ γ_t)^{-1} Σ γ_t f(x_t)  [Jensen's inequality]

interpretation.first_term: E[V(x_s, x*)] — initial Bregman distance to optimum, vanishes with distance-to-solution
interpretation.second_term: (M² + σ²) Σγ_t² / Σγ_t — variance floor from stochastic noise and gradient magnitude
interpretation.optimal_constant_step: Set γ_t = γ = D / (M√T) where D² = V(x_1, x*), gives O(1/√T) rate
interpretation.comparison_to_subgradient: When v(x) = ‖x‖₂²/2, V(x,z) = ‖x-z‖²/2, this reduces to the standard stochastic subgradient bound

new_lib_required: Bregman divergence V(x,z) definition and basic properties
patterns_used: bt - at²/2 ≤ b²/(2a) (AM-GM / completing the square)
proof_dependency_summary.critical_difference_from_subgradient: The three-point lemma replaces the norm expansion step. There is no inner_neg flip needed because the prox-mapping optimality condition directly gives the right sign.

formalization_roadmap.phase_1: Build Lib/Layer0/BregmanDivergence.lean: define V, prove V ≥ 0, prove three-point identity (3.2.6), prove Lemma 3.4.
formalization_roadmap.phase_2: Build Lib/Glue/MirrorDescentGlue.lean: noise cancellation lemma (E[<δ_t, x_t - x>] = 0), variance bound glue.
formalization_roadmap.phase_3: Write Algorithms/MirrorDescent.lean: define MirrorDescentSetup structure, define process via prox-mapping, state and prove Theorem 4.1.
formalization_roadmap.note: Phase 1 is the blocker. Without a Bregman divergence library, the proof cannot be written.
